description: Registry of optional codebase-analysis backends. STATIC metadata only (no source, no install). CodeBase Analyzer reads this to render the guided-install menu at point of need; live availability is resolved per backend via its validator. 'blessed' = vendored stub + validator, preferred. 'candidate' = clone/audit-only, gitignored, opt-in. Nothing here is installed automatically.

recommendation_tiers.tier_1_rationale: one tool per orthogonal paradigm at lowest operating cost: deep-structural + architecture (codebase-memory-mcp), fast-structural callers/impact (codegraph), natural-language/semantic (understand-anything).
recommendation_tiers.tier_2_rationale: more specialized and heavier: whole-repo path/community analysis (graphify), LSP-exact precision (serena). Add when you need their specific strength.
recommendation_tiers.always_on: rg is the always-fresh literal/fallback baseline and is not installable (always present).

requirements.notes: persistent local knowledge graph via CLI/MCP

cost.footprint: managed checkout + built binary + local cache

recommendation.good_at: The deepest structural backend: follows transitive call chains across multiple hops and surfaces architecture/cluster structure. Most capable overall and session-integrated; the finicky Cypher query surface is the trade-off.

Strong for reachability, shortest-path, and whole-repo community/cluster reports. Heavier and slower to build (minutes on a large repo), so reach for it specifically when you need path or community analysis.

cost.footprint: npm deps + dist/ build; index written to <target>/.codegraph/

recommendation.good_at: The fastest, lowest-friction structural tool: exact direct callers and change-impact from a quick CLI index, no API key. Best first graph backend for 'who calls this' and 'what breaks if I change it'.

requirements.notes: LSP-exact declarations/references via MCP stdio; reached through the mcp stdio probe

cost.footprint: python env + one language server per language

recommendation.good_at: Highest precision: real LSP declarations/references, exact even with overloaded or aliased names. Heaviest to operate (per-language servers, interactive setup), so use it when exactness matters more than setup cost.

backends.status_check: validator not yet built; verify integrations/serena/upstream/scripts/mcp_server.py exists, else treat as unavailable

requirements.llm_for_enrichment: any capable model, provider-agnostic (local or hosted); the eval used Gemini 3.1 Pro
requirements.api_key: only if the chosen LLM provider requires one
requirements.notes: tree-sitter graph + LLM enrichment; ADS wrapper scripts (build-graph/search-graph/enrich-graph-batch.mjs) are not yet tracked

cost.privacy: enrichment calls an external or local LLM (provider you choose)

recommendation.good_at: The natural-language/semantic winner: finds 'the feature that does X' by meaning when you don't know the symbol name. Needs a one-time LLM enrichment pass (any capable model), then pinpoints conceptually. NOTE: installer is Phase 3 (no validator yet) — set up manually for now.

backends.status_check: validator not yet built; verify build-graph.mjs + .understand-anything/knowledge-graph.json exist, else treat as unavailable
